name: Bushra Amiwala
subject: IL-9 raised me. Now I’m running to represent it.
body:
While some of my opponents might view this seat as an opportunity for a promotion, media attention or to gain more followers on social media, I view this seat as an opportunity to give back
| | | | | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wMyQQtfZrFd1BecTwqxtt7P86Ey0rq6jHi4SGD_zzoFWikD72W-g-ihOmKlDsxkYAOxE4BqrLpDcMmbmRZoEV8uzo6D3mojpnlUGiepydaLXezPOdENXq98ns964-ACJCbwqf40AgImqkUZU8y6wyLKlz4qzpQIUeWB4GTpc1qfhIuhf0UwGIQReOOBL5FHODMgiwfpB9YWhkjoUARtv4_r9eu9n0Pn1&c=LVFI9Z70pKDPhc5EOj9KMUVumUabW6zkc4Q1cfzYVtBq9zMYIKxOkQ==&ch=flOL9SaJokveWLtyr_0KASE_jqPn9c5nS2klurwBmgn0Pt_tKAn6zg==)
---
|
Peter,
Since the beginning of this campaign, I have been overwhelmed by the support of friends, family, neighbors, and supporters. My campaign has serious momentum, and that's because the people I am running to represent know me and what I stand for:
---
| | [](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wMyQQtfZrFd1BecTwqxtt7P86Ey0rq6jHi4SGD_zzoFWikD72W-g-ihOmKlDsxkYAOxE4BqrLpDcMmbmRZoEV8uzo6D3mojpnlUGiepydaLXezPOdENXq98ns964-ACJCbwqf40AgImqkUZU8y6wyLKlz4qzpQIUeWB4GTpc1qfhIuhf0UwGIQReOOBL5FHODMgiwfpB9YWhkjoUARtv40K9D0BjCTaO&c=LVFI9Z70pKDPhc5EOj9KMUVumUabW6zkc4Q1cfzYVtBq9zMYIKxOkQ==&ch=flOL9SaJokveWLtyr_0KASE_jqPn9c5nS2klurwBmgn0Pt_tKAn6zg==)
---
| This campaign isn't some vanity project for me -- I have been fighting against MAGA and for the people of this district since I was old enough to vote, and I have spent the last seven years working to make my community's public schools one the best places for children to get an education as a member of the Skokie School District 73.5 Board of Education.
While some of my opponents might view this seat as an opportunity for a promotion, media attention or to gain more followers on social media, I view this seat as an opportunity to continue giving back to the community that raised me and improving my neighbors lives with an agenda focused on their needs -- Medicare for All, cancelling student debt, making housing affordable, and much, much more.
That is why I am asking for your support so I can continue my work to help those who need it the most. [Will you help me keep the momentum by chipping in $50, $25 or even $5 so I can win this seat for the people of IL-9?](https://e96gjzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001wMyQQtfZrFd1BecTwqxtt7P86Ey0rq6jHi4SGD_zzoFWikD72W-g-ihOmKlDsxkYAOxE4BqrLpDcMmbmRZoEV8uzo6D3mojpnlUGiepydaLXezPOdENXq98ns964-ACJCbwqf40AgImqkUZU8y6wyLKlz4qzpQIUeWB4GTpc1qfhIuhf0UwGIQReOOBL5FHODMgiwfpB9YWhkjoUARtv41yPyvnb7ICx&c=LVFI9Z70pKDPhc5EOj9KMUVumUabW6zkc4Q1cfzYVtBq9zMYIKxOkQ==&ch=flOL9SaJokveWLtyr_0KASE_jqPn9c5nS2klurwBmgn0Pt_tKAn6zg==)

body:

| Hi Friend,

You’re feeling it every day: fossil fuels are driving the climate crisis and making life more expensive for all of us.

Power bills keep climbing. Insurance rates keep rising. And extreme weather is contributing to the exploding costs of groceries.

**None of this is natural. It’s the price of an energy system that puts fossil fuels ahead of people.**

And right now, the Trump regime is doing everything in their power (and, let’s be real, even some things that are beyond their legal power) to lock in the dangerous and outdated supremacy of fossil fuels for years and decades to come. They’re cancelling climate investments, handing more power over to their oil-drenched friends in the fossil fuel industry, and flagrantly disregarding public health and wellbeing.

**It’s times like these when people who care—people like you—make the difference.**

Your action today matters. When you donate to Evergreen Action, you join thousands of others who are fighting back by accelerating climate progress at the state level, where real wins are still within reach.

Your gift pushes forward policies that expand affordable clean energy like wind and solar, cut energy costs for families, and build a safer, healthier future. [**This is your chance to make a direct impact—right now, when it counts most.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4269433/87SdQ4wZ)

Tomorrow is Giving Tuesday, but you don’t have to wait to support the policies and advocacy that are moving real progress at the state level, in spite of Trump. [**Chip in today.**](https://actnow.evergreenaction.com/s/4269434/87SdQ4wZ) |

# [Stop Trump's rush to war with Venezuela! ](https://default.salsalabs.org/T0e1b09c7-b89d-451c-bdf7-195bef638f55/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | [](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tc18dfb56-0d1d-498e-b2b9-84ce393013a0/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)
---
| | | _(Note: you can also discuss this action on[our new Substack page](https://default.salsalabs.org/T75283c87-491c-43a1-91b6-902a70bbee20/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9))_ Donald Trump is rushing us to war with Venezuela, and we need to stop him. There is no reason for it, they represent zero threat to us, and Trump’s arguments for it seem to be based on ignorance and delusion. **[Add your name, and tell Trump that America is opposed to this rush to war. And if he won’t stop it, Congress needs to use the War Powers Act to stop it for him.](https://default.salsalabs.org/Tea02fca2-7fe3-40e2-b862-662e6dfc8bd7/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)** _(Already took this action? Share this with others, and help build momentum!)_ Trump has been blowing up boats without any proof of threat or wrongdoing, he sent forces into the area, and he openly been talking about an invasion. This would be a disaster. Venezuela is a major producer and exporter of oil (and an OPEC nation), and an attack would likely send oil prices skyrocketing. It could also spark a refugee crisis throughout the region, spurring instability that could lead to other wars. And it could lead to thousands or tens of thousands of deaths. No one wants this, and we shouldn’t stand by and let it happen. **[Don’t let Trump stupid-walk us into another unnecessary war. Add your name, and tell Trump: “No war with Venezuela!”](https://default.salsalabs.org/T98b1cccd-4619-4b3b-9dc8-d1b05f4ad271/368d17ff-e836-4168-8049-c5f0900fbbf9)**

subject: Snowstorm Snarls Travel, Booker Ties the Knot, and Snowy Owls Arrive
body:
[Record-breaking snowfall](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGSniAQwPGnwQ4HFkVTUNjYXYo0KR2-Y1XyITiwkfHtM949Qtr_zuzsb39_LB_WBx-3n_ZAQ2taItYSkAjzUmKqhVI-SrQ5FMr2wvBK6b2AgJ5L4EIuzvpwL13jzIoIqBo0ctBdPw5S62Y33eheCmGVoh9H_VodIthBOwcKhBpl482zTYKQUgotVNv1IIUeUA0_HHyqnnUCnaeU2z_JR9pxDXe6MLef6WiC2YnOwtTEYGYw11rbo8RnxmDGyP-Wp6KlHTODmdL5kDw--UyFOO02vsvmLx83Xn0kzPxLzb_Z_NvNI9ocbj5yIQRfg992KtxhsDc6BrPNfJrkr_yuU5ONO6sPwRc9sE5sh_Xh61r6rxdfBv4FAAD___8Rksw) in the Midwest and Great Lakes over the weekend led to widespread travel disruptions after Thanksgiving, including 8,00 delayed flights and another 670 cancellations.

| | |

| [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuu3CAQQL8Gd7Z4GbgFRaSr7W7alIg14_UkPCzAXu3fR95E-YG0c45mRufHl_vyGDE_vvsEtq_FZXi2CL1DdS2XZ-ulppZ9ja1Xf0K8l_LLccrnkfGRMhc8xpeTQ7ArAAcxgGVaydloptSw2TlQswovAjVAF6X1SiVnxkiuV-OBDmivbYxTxhhVVExy5owqDUJ_BL6ImUgKAXup08-CuW-wxlc5oU5LSUO0W-97I-Ib4TfCb5hPaH1KGErF8Y7lsi4kbkdPrpWjLkDEJySPkXB1DRMEPBIRn7vHMO6-9gy1bbj_5YtPu8dHfhv_IDN0FLNwfzIcGbt3TAmupPnQTA3Vhv2JMWJTmkj6uA6-X-7_1fm0_HcAAAD__xOKkWU) | |
# Monday, December 1, 2025
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1955, [Rosa Parks was arrested](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOtCAUBeCnwQ7DRcSxoPgbu_nbLQ3Idbw7CBNgx_j2Gzf7Blt_yTkn5-M-3y0Fio__dkdT1zRHPErAWjHPJaaj1JT3Em0OpWb7xuBSes5SyJ6D5AJmbymcs2q8WREldg0aGLTqbwNo3WxmHLXz4MUKgKrXehxuIzjXi25dnVqwIXOlgRQAILToWtVLEHrAbhi9XLqeKYGeasrtZ6JYN1zDmd6Y2yXtTTBbra_Cun9MTkxOx3G0G12rz8uZnOpGhXt7cor8V5icPC64O8wcmJxyKpa_bH4WTo9IFQt3X4W7dC6pNtn410EhUNEDU-KxWwo_3fVPh72N_A4AAP__Jy6BSA) in Montgomery, Alabama, for refusing to give up her seat to a white man on a segregated bus. Her quiet defiance led to the Montgomery Bus Boycott, a 382-day protest that crippled the city's transit system and vaulted 26-year-old Martin Luther King Jr. into national leadership. [Two snowy owls](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0EFyhCAQBdDT6A6LblDGBYts3E22WVqMtEqmFQuosbx9apIj5AL___e_7uPdBQ778uk2smWO405nZiqF0pj3eOYS05Z3lziX5F7EjxifI0psBaCQMHoX-Bp17e1MhKRqsmA63d4MdF292nZC0sqoeZYEXmrV9zeYbwQPb9peYx3sOw1QAoDspGp0iyA7Q8r0HifVVlqSDyWm5juGvaw08xVflJopbjXbtZQjV-qjwqHC4Viv3MS0VDi8JRUOf1tBHC4k8SZdIp6cBbsniS1Ma1jc3qxl4zpZf5yBOeTOVFoumwv821L-dc3L4k8AAAD__08Pd4g) just touched down in Chicago--weeks ahead of schedule--and drew more than 100 birdwatchers to Montrose Point as we cover in today's _Flyover_. If you're feeling restless this Monday, you're not the only one thinking about migrating south. 🎧 First Lady Melania Trump is stepping into Hollywood with the launch of her new production company, just days before her self-titled documentary hits theaters. Ayla Brown has the full lowdown in today's edition of [_The Flyover Podcast_](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykzztuAyEQANDTsJ1XMMN-KCgiRds5SpOkXGEz2MQsWDC25dtHyRVygSe9r_26dzHFfHpzG1kOZc30aImYqa4tl0fjUreWXU2Nq7tTOpRyWUHCsFOwk2r1LqbnqjtvAxEQdmTVNOphntQ4dmeLs9GTGwwa5zRoh94fQJOHMIQwG9NF-6spkEopOUrs9QBKjhPhZDwccRBako9cav9dYuYzhfQsd6r9sWxdsmfmaxP4ImARsDzLjW_9gQQs7xkvn7ePiPNR4MICX8Horlp_fcSUYhsnoeVpczH9Sfyv_t3CTwAAAP__0whs7g). **💸** Inbox alert later today: Our partner, [GoldCo](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkT3P1DAQhH-N0_nk7yRFClCIaF4QFaKKfPYmMfgjsn2J7t-jgAAhCop32515dlbz-WV-0c67uH7QAYa6pDnCWTzUCnkuMZ2lphxK1NmXmvUB_p7St5kRJjFlmNDZauefs2jssAAw4A0MtFVCdi1VqtkGughjle1Nx0knWiO5lqZfVH_vQQslGjdcNMoIpZQowm9CMkpUC7ztLTNcIkHAupry7WtysW6w-Gc6IN9MCo0ftlr3gvgbxCbEpvM8b3tOFUx1cQ3Pog8X14IhaOcvB2LTmrzFBza6bJgw1jJ5eflkIuJjJyVWvcSiZwQxpcOO-FtfEB8JofjdhcHTzwB4BOuMrmB_C8tinEV8bAn9tJFrlo_vv_wBPWqYjQ67dmv8jyyAdY-A-Pjj5l-rkh7ZAOLjP0GaPNj9dN67olokyPrr7aa-qt1jYN8DAAD__5zJtFE), is sending a quick breakdown of how cash and hard assets have performed over time, and why that gap matters now. If you're looking for an investment that tackles a massive global problem while tapping into explosive demand, today's sponsor, [Midori Bio's](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuu3CAQQL8Gd7Z4GbgFRaSr7W7alIg14_UkPCzAXu3fR95E-YG0c45mRufHl_vyGDE_vvsEtq_FZXi2CL1DdS2XZ-ulppZ9ja1Xf0K8l_LLccrnkfGRMhc8xpeTQ7ArAAcxgGVaydloptSw2TlQswovAjVAF6X1SiVnxkiuV-OBDmivbYxTxhhVVExy5owqDUJ_BL6ImUgKAXup08-CuW-wxlc5oU5LSUO0W-97I-Ib4TfCb5hPaH1KGErF8Y7lsi4kbkdPrpWjLkDEJySPkXB1DRMEPBIRn7vHMO6-9gy1bbj_5YtPu8dHfhv_IDN0FLNwfzIcGbt3TAmupPnQTA3Vhv2JMWJTmkj6uA6-X-7_1fm0_HcAAAD__xOKkWU) breakthrough tech for rapidly breaking down plastic waste is an opportunity worth paying attention to.
|
---
## Massive Snowstorm Delays Thanksgiving Travel
[Record-breaking snowfall](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGSniAQwPGnwQ4HFkVTUNjYXYo0KR2-Y1XyITiwkfHtM949Qtr_zuzsb39_LB_WBx-3n_ZAQ2taItYSkAjzUmKqhVI-SrQ5FMr2wvBK6b2AgJ5L4EIuzvpwL13jzIoIqBo0ctBdPw5S62Y33eheCmGVoh9H_VodIthBOwcKhBpl482zTYKQUgotVNv1IIUeUA0_HHyqnnUCnaeU2z_JR9pxDXe6MLef6WiC2YnOwtTEYGYw11rbo8RnxmDGyP-Wp6KlHTODmdL5kDw--UyFOO02vsvmLx83Xn0kzPxLzb_Z_NvNI9ocbj5yIQRfg992KtxhsDc6BrPNfJrkr_yuU5ONO6sPwRc9sE5sh_Xh61r6rxdfBv4FAAD___8Rksw) in the Midwest and Great Lakes over the weekend led to widespread travel disruptions after Thanksgiving, including nearly 8,000 delayed flights and another 670 cancellations. Chicago recorded [its highest-ever November snowfall](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSrCAQRb8GMy26UfAFBC8hm003tFDbkV2EKegda_5-y_mFzW_dU-d83qabDzGk-4c_yPKWp0RnjcRMZaopn5VzOWryJVYu_klxzvl7QolDC9hKmFYf4mvqm9VuREiqIQtG98NoQOtmt0pt4yw3MMrPIwxGaYOeUPVS6XHW1AR7vQFKAJBaqq4fEKQ2pMy_FRc1iF7SGjiX7iuHxDtt8ZWfVLolH020O_OjCvVfoBPolj0s_p67-pM4HFSvkUB3kuedikB3wQQ6AIFOSYHusmzfmm2hJZe1KXZ9nCHGULURvbwfPsQ3jP9U6GnxNwAA___8qHiz), with over 8 inches falling through Sunday morning. Additional severe weather is expected today as the system moves eastward, where slippery road conditions are likely to continue hampering drivers. Airlines also reported that a [mandatory software update](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFutDAQBeDTmA5kD8bsFi7-hm7_NiUa8ADODvbKtkB7-4hEygXSjTTSe_rex2N8oGcf1v-4ky1LHAOdmakUSmMO8cwlpj0HTJxLwoN4ivE5goSuVlBLNTr0_B515exCBNRWZFVvdHfrlTHVZtGoWfaaUJmbItKI4G6m7_Rk7ouausrbK02BVEpJI9tGd6Ck6ant7w7mthNakvMlpuYz-lA2WvgdD0rNHPeK7VbKK4v2n4BBwHCeZzNP-VJcfwHDdQoYyobhmVd_-LDWP5R6Yb9upXbE-M71jGEmZiw-hlz_4gUMVbLudXpmn00vtFx39PxdX_602WHhKwAA__8uP4Ng) caused additional delays for Airbus A320 flights worldwide. Most of the updates were completed yesterday.
## Lane Kiffin Chooses LSU, Leaves Ole Miss
Famed college football coach [Lane Kiffin is leaving Ole Miss](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu4yAQRb8Gd0QwNrZTuNiG7m27pUVgeGGDmQgmsfL3K_YXXjWj0ehenfPna_9yKafy_dsduHGkveDZMjJj3VuhszHVoxVXc-Pq3phvRI8dFBipQSq9B5fyZ5-GsEVEwHHATS_zZNZFz_Nw35SKYHyczKxCvK3T7EevV7O6ZVUmahjS1tM0KK21mtV4mQxoNS84LtcAfjRiUhgSU738pVT4jjF_6I314ukY8nZnfjYx_hJgBdjzPC-v5piC-_QHAbYjfPp8UuUmwBbvXOwH9AJsbxdgte77VYDNrqB8pBhTkd2G9OT8XTZkySQD-hRQ3pBPxCIpozxSa5KqzO0lIxHfXM7y9QyOUYBdFwPTeB2VWgTYoW7heaacU5sXManvw6X8n4R_pP-9wb8AAAD__xExl7s) to become the new head coach at Louisiana State University. He takes over for Brian Kelly, who was fired in October. Kiffin’s new deal reportedly makes him one of the highest-paid coaches in the sport. The move comes two days after Ole Miss defeated rivals Mississippi State to finish the regular season 11-1, and presumably secure the program’s [first-ever appearance](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGu3CAQRb8Gd6xgjI23oEhD99KmtLAZ1pM3hhWQtfbvo80vpL7S1Tnn19f6FYgpP36GE11PZc14Ncbesa4tl6v1Us-WQ-XWa3ghb6V8r6Bgkhqk0msMxO_VDNElRMBxQKftbKbF6nkeDqeX6Q7J4LYYWLZRbcberZm2Je5JxTAO5D5vGpTWWs1qvJkJtJotjvYeYR8nYRRG6qXefhfK_cDE7_LCetvLObA7en82Mf4Q4AX467pu2J75Mwrwe2HGB8pUSt8CswD_MXoL8KsAT1GAN1ZPZtQgwHPIKL8pJcryoIpRcvsj9xL2QybK1A7ZMLSSZWGUJ7U2VBefFzFTm60w6nEG4n9k_b9yvhz8DQAA__8y8YmE) in the College Football Playoff. Ole Miss officials declined to let Kiffin coach the football team through a [potential run](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0b2unDAQxfGngc7IHvNxt6BIQ3fTpkQGj8G5Yw-yJ7vZt4-I8gbpfzrSX-fH5_rpIsV8fHcJZwm8ZnxVQhEsa838qsIl1ewKVSnuibQxf62gYVAGlDard5Hea9_6OSAC2hZnM4398DGZcWzPeTQhYIANEf2Hs_oB3j4e4I3zFh3aNs73mgFtjNGjtl0_gNHjhHZ6eNjt0PQafRQu3U-OWU4M9OYnlm7n1NJ8ily1sd8aWBpY6sVFavd2J_MNGlh2JsIDVWCWzRE1sGwF3VfMh7prG1hckbgTNrCQy6i-Yggxq51TilKVsNpQUf1VVcbfonZ2-6kuFFQHk793rsKJBf1tLyzJZcyiTnT-n3aimFClWKsCrQdjwZrulERtmf31ikSxjlPT6yO5SH_b5L8Oec7wJwAA__9-iaRR) at the national title—which would also be the school’s first—after he chose to leave for an in-conference rival. Ole Miss defensive coordinator [Pete Golding](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO3CAURb8Gd1jwsHGmoEjjbtOmtBh42GSfeRaQsfbvo8kvbH91dc75_bF9-Ey57L_8ia4n3grejbB3rFsrfLfO9WzFV2q9-hfSk_lzAwWz1CCV3qLP9LVNQ3QJEdAM6PRip_nHoq0dDveYwZjFAxiTHhgTxGiDiWGBEKwOdsju_aZBaa2VVWacZtDKLmiWR4RgZjEpjLlzHf9wLv3ARF_8wjoGPgdyR-9XE-angFXAet_3GJ6tXVx7ey8ErIGJcEeZmPvTEwlY35YCViaUZ25NHrlikxd2lDtTzGWXB_ooA_twSPIF5WdOKRdJ7a-AdaguXncmys0uYlL76TP9B-rfqvhy8C8AAP__Qd2I2g) was immediately promoted to head coach—with no interim tag—following Kiffin’s decision.
## Couple Arrested for Using Spy Gear in Casino
A couple from Kazakhstan has landed in an Australian jail after allegedly using spy gear and winning over $1 million at a Sydney casino. See the [photos of the gear here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHO2yAURp8GbzeCi7H_f_CQqvKWDu3Q0bqBS0KDwQUSK336yn2Fzkf69J3z87JcKMSQbt9o5an5vCTea-TWuCw15b22XNaaqMTaCr04XnN-LCjRgEKQanEU4nvpOzd5ZmTd8aTGoTcfoxqG7j6RwasyVkrvyXjDVksj-8F_jP6q5Th2YTrWFEqllBykPvUGlRxG1uOnQ6uN6CW70HI5_cohtTv7-M4vLieb1y5O99a2KvRZ4Cxw3vf9tNZ0MIEzJ3hWgfMhdcBcohM4P-gPPe6wUShA1j4rO8genjWkG9TtbWkFSg6YyhbYcoWWYQ8JCBTgCiGBpRpSFjhTgfNZff99xk-h52yDE_rrl6Mo7_UHl60rk9v2EGOowyh6eVspxH_f238Ff034NwAA__-j1JVs). Casino staff noticed a small camera [concealed in the stitching](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGO4yAYBOCnwR0W_NhwKSiucZdrr7R-m5-YDYYISLzZp195X2HrkT7NzP_rfMUQQ7r9w51s83lOdNRIrVGZa8pHbbnsNWGJtRV8UVxyvs8gYOQSuJCzwxDf89A564mAVEdWGj2Mf4zUutssSm0WbwaFTjkH-mKEVAZQ-8WvoHQX7KlJEFJKoYXqhxGk0IaUuThY1cgGQS60XPqPHFLbyMd3flHp17x30W6tPSpTfxlMDKbjOPq61DPr8clgOvcwmLC0sEZiMN3xC-9bbZj4mp-PSBxLodrI8VPlGCPdyHG58xVrSJnXFXcG0-BMu_hPn7pi3eMIMYaqDRvEbccQf9q0X134svAdAAD__yTQhyI) of 36-year-old Dilnoza Israilova’s Mickey Mouse T-shirt, leading to the arrest of her and her husband, Alisherykhoja Israilov, 44. When searched, police found magnetized probes and a phone mirror attachment in the possession of the couple. Further searches, including the pair’s hotel room, revealed earpieces used for receiving tips on when to bet on card games. The Israilovas applied for casino membership on the same day they arrived in Sydney from Kazakhstan in October and raised suspicion by winning approximately $1.2 million over several visits.

### A Genius Way to Eliminate 400M Tons of Plastic Waste
Over 400 million tons of plastic are produced yearly. [Midori Bio](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuu3CAQQL8Gd7Z4GbgFRaSr7W7alIg14_UkPCzAXu3fR95E-YG0c45mRufHl_vyGDE_vvsEtq_FZXi2CL1DdS2XZ-ulppZ9ja1Xf0K8l_LLccrnkfGRMhc8xpeTQ7ArAAcxgGVaydloptSw2TlQswovAjVAF6X1SiVnxkiuV-OBDmivbYxTxhhVVExy5owqDUJ_BL6ImUgKAXup08-CuW-wxlc5oU5LSUO0W-97I-Ib4TfCb5hPaH1KGErF8Y7lsi4kbkdPrpWjLkDEJySPkXB1DRMEPBIRn7vHMO6-9gy1bbj_5YtPu8dHfhv_IDN0FLNwfzIcGbt3TAmupPnQTA3Vhv2JMWJTmkj6uA6-X-7_1fm0_HcAAAD__xOKkWU) found a way to turn that waste into a gold mine. Their proprietary technology helps plastic break down 64 times faster, creating biogas that can be used as clean energy from existing landfills, called Advanced BioRecycleTM. It’s a [special ingredient](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuu3CAQQL8Gd7Z4GbgFRaSr7W7alIg14_UkPCzAXu3fR95E-YG0c45mRufHl_vyGDE_vvsEtq_FZXi2CL1DdS2XZ-ulppZ9ja1Xf0K8l_LLccrnkfGRMhc8xpeTQ7ArAAcxgGVaydloptSw2TlQswovAjVAF6X1SiVnxkiuV-OBDmivbYxTxhhVVExy5owqDUJ_BL6ImUgKAXup08-CuW-wxlc5oU5LSUO0W-97I-Ib4TfCb5hPaH1KGErF8Y7lsi4kbkdPrpWjLkDEJySPkXB1DRMEPBIRn7vHMO6-9gy1bbj_5YtPu8dHfhv_IDN0FLNwfzIcGbt3TAmupPnQTA3Vhv2JMWJTmkj6uA6-X-7_1fm0_HcAAAD__xOKkWU) that can be added to any plastic manufacturing process with zero downtime at the factory and no capital expenditure. That’s why global brands like Head/Penn Tennis, Snibbs Footwear, and Planters Peanuts are already adopting Midori’s tech to meet their eco-pledges. And Midori is in talks with 150+ more potential partners. As plastic production is projected to triple by 2060, regulation and consumer demand will likely force more brands to act. That means now is the time to [invest in Midori Bio as they look towards a planned Nasdaq listing](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLuu3CAQQL8Gd7Z4GbgFRaSr7W7alIg14_UkPCzAXu3fR95E-YG0c45mRufHl_vyGDE_vvsEtq_FZXi2CL1DdS2XZ-ulppZ9ja1Xf0K8l_LLccrnkfGRMhc8xpeTQ7ArAAcxgGVaydloptSw2TlQswovAjVAF6X1SiVnxkiuV-OBDmivbYxTxhhVVExy5owqDUJ_BL6ImUgKAXup08-CuW-wxlc5oU5LSUO0W-97I-Ib4TfCb5hPaH1KGErF8Y7lsi4kbkdPrpWjLkDEJySPkXB1DRMEPBIRn7vHMO6-9gy1bbj_5YtPu8dHfhv_IDN0FLNwfzIcGbt3TAmupPnQTA3Vhv2JMWJTmkj6uA6-X-7_1fm0_HcAAAD__xOKkWU).

On this day in 1955, in Montgomery, Alabama, for refusing to give up her seat to a white man on a segregated bus. Her quiet defiance led to the Montgomery Bus Boycott, a 382-day protest that crippled the city's transit system and vaulted 26-year-old Martin Luther King Jr. into national leadership. just touched down in Chicago--weeks ahead of schedule--and drew more than 100 birdwatchers to Montrose Point as we cover in today's Flyover. Ayla Brown has the full lowdown in today's edition of . 💸 Inbox alert later today: Our partner, , is sending a quick breakdown of how cash and hard assets have performed over time, and why that gap matters now. If you're looking for an investment that tackles a massive global problem while tapping into explosive demand, today's sponsor, breakthrough tech for rapidly breaking down plastic waste is an opportunity worth paying attention to. | Massive Snowstorm Delays Thanksgiving Travel in the Midwest and Great Lakes over the weekend led to widespread travel disruptions after Thanksgiving, including nearly 8,000 delayed flights and another 670 cancellations. Chicago recorded , with over 8 inches falling through Sunday morning. Additional severe weather is expected today as the system moves eastward, where slippery road conditions are likely to continue hampering drivers. Airlines also reported that a caused additional delays for Airbus A320 flights worldwide. Most of the updates were completed yesterday. Lane Kiffin Chooses LSU, Leaves Ole Miss Famed college football coach to become the new head coach at Louisiana State University. He takes over for Brian Kelly, who was fired in October. Kiffin’s new deal reportedly makes him one of the highest-paid coaches in the sport. The move comes two days after Ole Miss defeated rivals Mississippi State to finish the regular season 11-1, and presumably secure the program’s in the College Football Playoff. Ole Miss officials declined to let Kiffin coach the football team through a at the national title—which would also be the school’s first—after he chose to leave for an in-conference rival. Ole Miss defensive coordinator was immediately promoted to head coach—with no interim tag—following Kiffin’s decision. Couple Arrested for Using Spy Gear in Casino A couple from Kazakhstan has landed in an Australian jail after allegedly using spy gear and winning over $1 million at a Sydney casino. See the . Casino staff noticed a small camera of 36-year-old Dilnoza Israilova’s Mickey Mouse T-shirt, leading to the arrest of her and her husband, Alisherykhoja Israilov, 44. When searched, police found magnetized probes and a phone mirror attachment in the possession of the couple. Further searches, including the pair’s hotel room, revealed earpieces used for receiving tips on when to bet on card games. The Israilovas applied for casino membership on the same day they arrived in Sydney from Kazakhstan in October and raised suspicion by winning approximately $1.2 million over several visits. | Greater Georgia Newsletter December 2025
---
| We trust you had a wonderful Thanksgiving!
From the Greater Georgia HQ, we share our gratitude for all that makes Georgia so special -- the remarkable people, caring leadership, and a community of conservatives committed to making our state better.
Simply put, we're grateful to YOU!
Now, with December underway, we're heading into a pivotal final stretch and we need your help: Join us as we inform voters of special elections, renewed voter registration efforts, and launch our final major canvassing event of the year.

|
## GOTV Impact Report
---
| In the run-up to the 2025 statewide and municipal elections, we focused GOTV efforts on the regions that needed it most. Because of your efforts, more than 84,000 conservatives were mobilized in a low-turnout election.
Of course, there's still more to do! With many run-offs being held tomorrow, and two special elections on the ballot December 9, we can keep growing our impact between now and the New Year.

## Georgia U.S. Senators Vote Against Govt. Reopening
---
|

name: Breaking
subject: Major escalation: Colorado joins redistricting fight
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fair Fight Colorado
body:
This is an existential crisis. Colorado must pick up the torch, join the fight, and meet this moment head on
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001QHWdRne4GoH-3DVjqZjZ3TPC-7mwxNl_PbJmSMkNXcB_z4v9U9jYnNDxbAl1NaZ7vLt3Fw4NsQEi0FpIE5Q3eGAwQspOvXy6BFLbx4hBiMGTTnQ1Y-51tGH1PVqcjgbRDyzcihQ6t7EbwxRF3WXhMYpfchc1-vTp11mj2VQqYs_nhK-JRCbZUmGAwKOzq5NdpG5ALltwIqZKzoemY4qvNj9hNkcFsk4H&c=CgRGaR23kZ3hzvhPnUp_bHyaOVmmzYdS6gGCmwBXwS7c0AoqF64GGw==&ch=2cUvBWN4kKQMiAcSU42IlPCyEplubqObI1kxWFK_-2TB_Jw8RRIDJQ==)
---
| Peter,
If Democrats reclaim the House next November the MAGA agenda hits a brick wall. Trump will become a lame duck president incapable of fulfilling any further policy goals. And right now a blue wave and a cataclysmic GOP defeat is exactly what we're on track for.
The only way MAGA can survive 2026 is if they rig the game.
Which is why in red states across the country, Trump is pushing for new congressional maps that will make up for the dozens of seats they are set to lose. A move that could establish one party rule for generations.
---
| | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001QHWdRne4GoH-3DVjqZjZ3TPC-7mwxNl_PbJmSMkNXcB_z4v9U9jYnNDxbAl1NaZ7vLt3Fw4NsQEi0FpIE5Q3eGAwQspOvXy6BFLbx4hBiMGTTnQ1Y-51tGH1PVqcjgbRDyzcihQ6t7EbwxRF3WXhMYpfchc1-vTp11mj2VQqYs_nhK-JRCbZUmGAwKOzq5NdpG5ALltwIqZgkHQS6pnoXrz23XAQqR0x&c=CgRGaR23kZ3hzvhPnUp_bHyaOVmmzYdS6gGCmwBXwS7c0AoqF64GGw==&ch=2cUvBWN4kKQMiAcSU42IlPCyEplubqObI1kxWFK_-2TB_Jw8RRIDJQ==)
---
The GOP is moving fast. Gerrymandered maps have passed in Texas, North Carolina, Missouri, and a push for redistricting is underway in Indiana and Florida with more red states soon to follow.

This is an existential crisis. No matter how unpopular Trump becomes, if left unchecked, MAGA will create enough new red seats to ensure their House majority is insurmountable.

However, Americans are pushing back. Last month, California fired the opening blow of the Democratic counter-salvo with their vote to pass Proposition 50, a ballot measure that gives CA a fair congressional map to counter Trump's gerrymandering push.

But California alone won't be enough. To save our Democracy, blue states like Colorado must pick up the torch, join the fight, and meet this moment head on.

This is why Fair Fight Colorado is proud to announce that we are introducing the ballot measure that will give every Coloradan a chance to vote on a new Congressional map.

We have the power to level the playing field and counter Trump's power grab with fair maps that will put power back in the hands of the people but it will require an astronomically expensive push to get this on the ballot and in front of Colorado voters.

MAGA is about to throw everything they've got into stopping us and we need to brace for impact. [Will you rush a contribution of $50, $25, or even $5, to become a founding donor and help Colorado fight fire with fire?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001QHWdRne4GoH-3DVjqZjZ3TPC-7mwxNl_PbJmSMkNXcB_z4v9U9jYnNDxbAl1NaZ7t1yGCV21BC2-s0vj7Ge-dFfOSN-gSa7YgguK-jnW4-XzllE5HX7M7qAXccnM51CEIUm9Sbb9-cX88DdnCyYeJs2rQc_tWpJyHDWqFCGBDtm-cSXkJ5Jc-VEWVWffOMO_pExIExRQ3h1wlX01e_800TJ1tgI-kNkRCEPUXhFIgrMvTHz2n_kvkQ==&c=CgRGaR23kZ3hzvhPnUp_bHyaOVmmzYdS6gGCmwBXwS7c0AoqF64GGw==&ch=2cUvBWN4kKQMiAcSU42IlPCyEplubqObI1kxWFK_-2TB_Jw8RRIDJQ==)

clean_body: This is an existential crisis. Colorado must pick up the torch, join the fight, and meet this moment head on | | | | | == | Peter, If Democrats reclaim the House next November the MAGA agenda hits a brick wall. Trump will become a lame duck president incapable of fulfilling any further policy goals. And right now a blue wave and a cataclysmic GOP defeat is exactly what we're on track for. The only way MAGA can survive 2026 is if they rig the game. Which is why in red states across the country, Trump is pushing for new congressional maps that will make up for the dozens of seats they are set to lose. A move that could establish one party rule for generations. | | == The GOP is moving fast. Gerrymandered maps have passed in Texas, North Carolina, Missouri, and a push for redistricting is underway in Indiana and Florida with more red states soon to follow. This is an existential crisis. No matter how unpopular Trump becomes, if left unchecked, MAGA will create enough new red seats to ensure their House majority is insurmountable. However, Americans are pushing back. Last month, California fired the opening blow of the Democratic counter-salvo with their vote to pass Proposition 50, a ballot measure that gives CA a fair congressional map to counter Trump's gerrymandering push. But California alone won't be enough. To save our Democracy, blue states like Colorado must pick up the torch, join the fight, and meet this moment head on. This is why Fair Fight Colorado is proud to announce that we are introducing the ballot measure that will give every Coloradan a chance to vote on a new Congressional map. We have the power to level the playing field and counter Trump's power grab with fair maps that will put power back in the hands of the people but it will require an astronomically expensive push to get this on the ballot and in front of Colorado voters. MAGA is about to throw everything they've got into stopping us and we need to brace for impact. == | | | | | == | == | == | | == | == | == | | | | Together we can fight back, make history, and ensure that Colorado plays its part in ensuring a fair election. Thank you for standing with us. Val Nosler Beck Executive Director Fair Fight Colorado | | | | | | | | | | == | | == | | | | Donate by mail: Fair Fight Colorado 1601 Arapahoe Street FL 10 Denver, Colorado 80202 | | | Paid for by Fair Fight Colorado. **Good Morning!** On this day in 1880, Jay Gould's [Missouri Pacific Railway Company](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL2uozAQRp_GdCAzOPwULrahy7ZbooEZwmgNRvbckLz9Ve4r3OKrPukc6fy7T3eUIMfjL-7s9bXG6eArB1blNCmnFNMZoq5RAtPnUlQOE1i4lTWUtp4IJbwnV5CfLQ11wb7uWncbrOubYvMDIcy8rgS27Zoe697RQMPazLaziF0h_gOrwdafAVSLdX17s9R3SDQ4Ms4yicZU6cbKL8xreMcnp2qJexH8pnpm0_wxMBoYr-uqNG8YjyAHVzE9DIwbHjTH-N_AyIcm4Wxg3CXn-JWkPHGRVZYyv7PyXiRP5yUhSG474-xjRwk_Kv1doKeH7wAAAP__nHh9GQ) began operating in Texas by leasing the Missouri-Kansas-Texas Railroad and its line from Kansas to Texas. U.S. Rep. Troy Nehls (R-Richmond) is retiring from Congress, but his twin brother is running to succeed him. Get [the details](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOpDAMBuCnCZ1HiScwUKS4hm6vvRIFYiBSSFDiZZa3P2VeYQtXv_TZ_v99TV_WBx-3v_Ygwz9rmiK9SyBmyhNTzimfIfGafCBXI7ZMYUKJLSgEqSZnfbgn3TgzSzeohox6dbodpO6fzW76tZvnbqVu0BrnQa-yXbvZOpStew56brypmEKp6iA-Fqn7rpWuf1nnBu2EluQ8p_zgnZh-bFnDnS7KjyUdTTA781nE84_AUeAY7zMVrpHAscoCR6UEjk8pcPwuUJ8QOH4gyHQC53RDpD0UCGQvHzdYUtwylQL89hHmnHinDJzpogw2xvQdFyqw2OO0fovAqUrBLgS7r4ubbNz59iH40r2Eltthffjcy79r-TL4PwAA__-KeZO5) in today's top _Roundup_ story. First Lady Melania Trump is stepping into Hollywood with the launch of her new production company, just days before her self-titled documentary hits theaters. ## Twin Running to Succeed Brother in Congress
U.S. Rep. Troy Nehls (R-Richmond) announced Saturday he is not running for reelection, becoming the [sixth Republican](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL_SoyAUxZ8GOzJwJYoFxTZ22XZLB-GqzCI4cJOYt__GvMJXnOo358-cf4_pYUMMaf1rdzR0LnlK-K4RibBMhKXkcsRMSw4R_YXIEsYJBNy5BC7k5G2In0k13szCD7JBI_tO3QehdNtsppOL1tbrVttOLHJwQ291L1s_KAGt6JtgrjAJQl4CuDmhdHcXXvfW-0F5pgT6QLncaEPC09YlfvILy83lvYlmIzoqa_8wGBmMdnayvQiD8ciVGIzPygsenEr-8IRbrLwi_ucFMaKjkBOf0eU9pJXXcNLGvyWX5znH4GziNqX8TA45noG4y2ktWCuDUWqATuuBwdgU4493iDHUrmdKrLsN8TuRfnfsy8BPAAAA___2A458) from Texas to announce retirement from Congress this cycle. The three-term congressman is a staunch Trump ally and has [backed proposals](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LGOpDAMBuCnCZ1HiScwUKS4hm6vvRIFYiBSSFDiZZa3P2VeYQtXv_TZ_v99TV_WBx-3v_Ygwz9rmiK9SyBmyhNTzimfIfGafCBXI7ZMYUKJLSgEqSZnfbgn3TgzSzeohox6dbodpO6fzW76tZvnbqVu0BrnQa-yXbvZOpStew56brypmEKp6iA-Fqn7rpWuf1nnBu2EluQ8p_zgnZh-bFnDnS7KjyUdTTA781nE84_AUeAY7zMVrpHAscoCR6UEjk8pcPwuUJ8QOH4gyHQC53RDpD0UCGQvHzdYUtwylQL89hHmnHinDJzpogw2xvQdFyqw2OO0fovAqUrBLgS7r4ubbNz59iH40r2Eltthffjcy79r-TL4PwAA__-KeZO5) to put the president's name on the $100 bill and rename Dulles International Airport after Trump. Hours after his announcement, his identical twin brother, Trever Nehls, said he was [running for the seat](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDuu4zAMRVcjdzYoWv4VKqZxl2mnNBSJjoWhI0Mm4mT3D8oWXkGAAIlzLu6_23JzkePz8dftZOW9puVJ18kkQnkRyjnlg5OsKTKFchInxAsCdrXGGvQSXOTPYqpg7xAmXZHVQ2-6CczYVpudQov96HEcPAwBWw9rGH3o9B18q72poi0wjaDLIDYezNh3EMbBhTCZoAxQiJJyIxsJvd258ie9KDc-7RXbTeQ4VftH4axwvq6rORJHiT6VB4Vzia1wLhqFs9ZlnxTOX1bN7trdf8r1kzY-a4B-gGHqqmzDcUXmePaDMvDYXeSvUX7X08viTwAAAP__boV6kw). "_District 22 needs a Representative who will follow in Troy's footsteps and stand shoulder-to-shoulder with President Trump_ ," [said Trever Nehls](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGuqzAMQL8GtqDEDaEMGd7C1re-EYXElOi5CUrc0v79Ff2FO1iydKyjI_-7zTcXKab7X_dAy-81zwmPSsiMZWYsJZedMq85EoYTsWOkGST0QoGQag4u0mfWbbCLDKNq0arB6H6U-nppNyvVIId-XY0fB61X0JfRuGFEqfQaFunbaE-ZAqnOAei81FfTy3AdXAijDo2WGCLn0vGGjG9XV_rkF5bO50dLdmPea3P508DUwHQcR5cWf6aevIFpzxQ5-trA5HO6F6zn-hWJLT8rioL7c6HoXRJHJBIps6iI_0VBgYSeY04CJBhRfHKgjTZ9W2zYz-tYzdBoeX-4SN8i_t0fXxZ-AgAA__84jIjZ) in his announcement. "_I'm ready to take up that fight_."
## Afghan National Arrested for Terroristic Threat
An Afghan national was arrested last week after he posted a TikTok video in which he [threatened to bomb](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDGOpDAQRU8DmZExBkPgYBOy3nRDVNgFlNZgZNc03bcf0XOECX70pK-n9-8xPYACHetf2NHya4nTgVcOyIxpYkwppjNEXiIF9DdiYAyTkqoVtRKynjxQeE-69HaWfqhLtLXpdDtI3TflZlXXNNKpQWHbm35wbl5Mveh50LMxs-lKsvdZrWR9T6nKSd13rfS9Ae8H7Qst0RPHVPGGjC_IS3jHJ6bKxb0MdmM-c9H8KdRYqPG6rmqJr1v15oUav3KhRljWDQ5xAFM8IAjwOzGjF08CEU9MHyAgBMIsLgwu7ijcBmlFL3b4T8cqfnpQZnKCt4TAwm9ZZHjnMll_XhQC5c4UWq47UPgI8u-yPq36DgAA__-0HpEZ) an unspecified target in Fort Worth. Mohammad Dawood Alokozay was [admitted to the country](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLFygzAQRL8GOpiTOIFUqEhD57QpGYU7Gc0I44iLsf8-g38hxVb7dndmvy7TJaScbtfPsLKXZ9ymGx97ZhEuk3ApW7nnTeKWMtNpSRDOkwZtGqUbUBOFlF8T1uS_gZyq2auhR-MAbVcvfrDYGzUgGAOI4IKKHBlUpM7Fmbo6-bNMaVCntG5nQNsbIDsEIodUITAl2UorCws_wx7za3twaedtrbNfRO571X1Ueqz0eBzHyc18Ey77z28ofHKVHt_RSo-hSJozT0EFctbEBgi6Bge0jbMzNWh0x4NFN1toF1lzXTzdj5Rz2vuhQriuIeX3uPzvsofXfwEAAP__5IJ-ig) after the 2021 U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan under the Biden administration's Operation Allies Welcome program. He was arrested by state and federal authorities on Tuesday, the day before another Afghan national attacked two National Guard soldiers in Washington, D.C. Alokozay is detained at the Tarrant County Jail on state charges of making a terroristic threat. ICE has [placed an immigration hold](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbHuozAMh58mbK6CGxIyMNzC9r_1RmSIgaiBVMEt7dufuFe4wYP12Z9s_f78DD8UU9yX37RxJ585DzufR2IRLoNwKbk8U5Y5x8ThQkLCaUCNDdQIuh4CxfQdTBW6UQdfV9zVzprGa9Peq7VjjdyiGy1y7UffeKLRkm-tri0R6yp2l6xGXV-FeJu0aW2jQ-soBG-CMppDlFxusrLwh445ffOby23KW5W6VeR5qPsvhb3C_jzP2zkTXVBhT0XilFhhfx2vsE95oqSwFyqFdoEpv3b5Xou5POK-wEY7nGuGZz6EA0h8SH7AOwbOcLyWhQ-5xlaGkw4YXzGFqx_zNoJkECoLC8y5CJy5yApUmIBK4cunsMfWARrbGDcHYEMNGN824CdLMAfnrHbe3e9UlS48z5hSPKxTRi8bxfTvZ_m_pN4d_g0AAP__FOKp0w) on him. "_The magnitude of the national security crisis Joe Biden unleashed on our country over the span of four years cannot be overstated_ ,” White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt [posted on social media](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDtuwzAQRE9DdTaWK4qfgkUadQ4CpEhJMOLKJkCbBrmx5dsHyhVSTDMDPAze1ymcYi75dn6PV_K8rTXc6NkLMVMLTK3Vdi-V15oLpX3iyFQCAk4HiQeQIcVcXkENyX9DcnIgL41WkwNlx-HikwaUkbQGR6OWo1HRRYAFQE7rMpoh-x0mEeQexOMCyuoJkjUxJaeSUEApc21HvhDTFvtaXvVB7bjU61D8hfnexfgmcBY4b3srcP5o1PsnLQLn_fNPFzhL55RF5SyA1QAazDg0n-7PXEru2ggF52vM5Q_M_9Px8PgbAAD__yQ9cN8) in reaction to Alokozay's arrest.
## New Laws Going Into Effect
Several new state laws passed during the second special session will [go into effect](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGOszAQhJ8GOtDaMQYKF3-TLn97JVrsdeLTgiOzCcnbn8g9whWrLWb0zWi-LtMFE6f1-h8XcvKKeVpp35hEqExCpeRy5ywxJ6ZwSIJCPGnQXaN0A2oKmPg9mTq4GcKoanKqt6YbwQyn-uagU2a2A44wq-gp2n62pxGjHcF33s91cgdMaVDHad16MIPtIAw9hjCaUBmgkCSXVm4k9MIt8js_qbQ-LzW7m8h9q07_Kn2u9Hnf9zbmV4-PTdJ6WCp9Por_vuYDaBj3rQnkaZmpNN-4PrC86-LCfU_MabN9ZeC6YOJPiPxtmqfTPwEAAP__fIN6kw) on Thursday, including the redistricting plan that would add five GOP-leaning seats to the state's congressional delegation. Alokozay is detained at the Tarrant County Jail on state charges of making a terroristic threat. ICE has on him. | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: If you bank anywhere in the U.S., this concerns you.| | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | Your Bank Could Be Closing | ---|--- | | If you bank anywhere in the U.S., this concerns you. Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. [Listen to post * 18:52](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=5156566&post_id=179681048&utm_source=podcast-email&play_audio=true&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-play-on-substack&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5NjgxMDQ4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ1OTM3ODYsImV4cCI6MTc2NzE4NTc4NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTUxNTY1NjYiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.v_cPGh-plyhcH6LeRAWE4-QPoWwKc4M2m-m4vtMzsC0&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email#play)
---
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/70fb33ec-038b-46e6-bed7-081610bd3285?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
[On the night of October 21st, 2025](https://substack.com/redirect/9641ac2a-e415-47d4-ad75-cf5379cc1bc8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), the Republican nominee for Virginia's Lieutenant Governor beamed behind a podium at an event he never thought would actually happen. After nearly 10 challenges to his opponent, Democratic Virginia State Senator Ghazala Hashmi, John Reid finally got the debate he'd been demanding for months--after being the only statewide race to not have one.
The two "candidates" traded turns answering questions in what was, on its face, one of the more cordial debates of the season. Reid laid out his conservative positions on book bans, LGBTQ rights, reproductive rights, and workers' rights. Hashmi's "responses" aligned cleanly with her actual record as a Democratic nominee.
The only problem was that Senator Hashmi wasn't in the room. In fact, she wasn't even participating.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/d611392c-4461-4b50-8002-edb31226f272?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Thanks for reading By the Ballot! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
[Subscribed](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuYnl0aGViYWxsb3QuY29tL2FjY291bnQiLCJwIjoxNzk2ODEwNDgsInMiOjUxNTY1NjYsImYiOnRydWUsInUiOjkwNTAxMTkyLCJpYXQiOjE3NjQ1OTM3ODYsImV4cCI6MjA4MDE2OTc4NiwiaXNzIjoicHViLTAiLCJzdWIiOiJsaW5rLXJlZGlyZWN0In0.o2cHK6tDDehMxqtooIZIErNGtNHlhIqdx66ZxTGDBuA?)
[Miles away in Fredericksburg,](https://substack.com/redirect/86a1154d-204b-4d18-bd66-3c6d121c7d82?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Hashmi was attending a stop on the "Bills Are Too Damn High" tour, an event hosted by Clean Virginia to highlight rising utility bills. She hadn't agreed to any debate at all.
With only 13 days left in the cycle--and desperate to avoid being the lone candidate without a single debate--Reid's campaign unveiled what they deemed "the next best alternative." A monitor stood on the podium across from him displaying a photo of Senator Hashmi. But the voice answering the questions did not belong to her, but to an AI-generated simulation built from her public statements and questionnaires.
While the words didn't technically misrepresent her platform, the stunt raised major questions about the use of artificial intelligence in politics--particularly around consent and manipulation of likeness. Debates are, by definition, mutual agreements. When one side writes the rules, picks the questions, and fabricates the participation of the other candidate, the event becomes little more than performance art.
One would expect some mechanism to regulate AI applications in campaigns. Instead, most states--including Virginia--have none. Despite multiple attempts in recent years to rein in the use of synthesized media, all have failed.
During the 2025 session alone, lawmakers attempted to address the issue. Senator Adam Ebbin introduced [SB571 ](https://substack.com/redirect/50ce3d1e-9299-4321-8a24-f47cc99b4c9d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)and Delegate Michelle Maldonado introduced [HB697](https://substack.com/redirect/67e6f208-161f-4a40-842f-36ff4106a429?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--bills that would have made it a Class I misdemeanor to use synthetic media for fraud, defamation, or libel by manipulating another person's voice or likeness. Both died in committee.
Senate Majority Leader Scott Surovell introduced [SB775,](https://substack.com/redirect/731c5382-b32c-459c-8ff9-3adb18349422?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and Delegate Mark Sickles carried its House companion, [HB2479](https://substack.com/redirect/e7c74723-6c3d-46c1-8fa8-77b52eaa3bdb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), requiring disclaimers on political ads that use synthetic media. A basic disclosure--simply acknowledging the use of AI. The legislature passed both. Governor Youngkin vetoed them, citing fears that the regulations were "too broad" and would stifle innovation.
Killing these bills opened the door for Reid's AI "debate" and others like it. Reid may not have crossed into outright fabrication or slander, but the line is perilously thin--and absolutely nothing in Virginia law prevents a future campaign from crossing it as the technology becomes more realistic.
Political operatives and candidates of all parties now have to confront the same question: **How much will innovation cost us in political integrity --and what are we willing to do to maintain the public's trust?**
## **Innovation before Integrity**
On his first day back in office, President Donald Trump signed an executive order establishing [America's AI Action Plan](https://substack.com/redirect/e7e42494-e7cf-47e9-a898-513e6695914c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). The order stripped away regulatory barriers, promoted open-source large language models, and fast-tracked data center permitting. But its most consequential--and often overlooked--provision prohibits states from regulating artificial intelligence at all.
Trump's directive argues for a "single federal standard" to avoid a patchwork of state rules and directs federal agencies to weigh a state's "AI regulatory environment" when allocating discretionary funds.
Soon after, [Congressman Michael Baumgartner of Washington introduced l](https://substack.com/redirect/fc5f5016-8f82-42fe-8e5a-9d5860247a49?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)egislation to codify the executive order and formally block states from imposing their own AI restrictions.
With the guardrails gone, AI development has exploded. The "AI slop" your parents share on Facebook has become disturbingly realistic. [You might remember the viral clip](https://substack.com/redirect/8c3709c6-db47-428e-9f02-bca5e42e91b3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of a woman smashing a glass bridge with a cartoonishly giant boulder. While ridiculous, thousands believed it--proving that realism can override reason. If absurd content can fool so many, imagine what happens when the videos are plausible and politically explosive.
AI content that was once easy to suss out has now taken an ultrarealistic face that many people with limited technological literacy-and unfortunately, a significant part of our nation's electorate-believe.
## **Political and Campaign Abuse**
During the 2025 cycle here in Virginia, we saw a small but telling preview of just how quickly this can spiral. Posts and replies on social media became breeding grounds for AI-driven misinformation. The Spanberger campaign even said they had to field angry calls from top donors and long-time supporters about an AI-generated video circulating online that featured Abigail Spanberger saying incredibly reprehensible things. The clip was entirely fabricated. And while X eventually removed it, the damage had already been done -- and the election had already passed.
Situations like this are only going to get worse and harder to detect as guardrails remain nonexistent and self-described "innovationphiles" insist that any regulation is an attack on progress. Here in Virginia, we're watching it unfold in real time as data centers grow like weeds throughout the Commonwealth. With rapid development comes more accessibility; with more accessibility comes more opportunities to both dupe and be duped by artificially generated content.
And this isn't isolated to state races. AI has already impacted the 2026 midterm elections in one of the most competitive U.S. Senate races in the country. On November 10, the [campaign account for Mike Collins](https://substack.com/redirect/dbf86712-0f2d-45a4-b541-9a88f157fe33?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) -- a Republican candidate for U.S. Senate in Georgia -- posted an AI-generated video of Senator Jon Ossoff after the Senate voted to reopen the government. Ossoff had voted against the bill due to the lack of a vote on ACA subsidies. The video, at first glance, looked incredibly realistic, showing Ossoff casually admitting that he voted against reopening the government despite hurting farmers and SNAP beneficiaries.
[](https://substack.com/redirect/b32c0397-d4f6-4290-ba0a-2ee8e25b33f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
And people bought it. A lot of people. Outrage piled up in the comments, accusing Ossoff of callousness, incompetence, and even corruption. Then the outrage shifted to Mike Collins after the video was debunked -- though by then, it had already done its damage.
The problem here isn't just ethical, though it is deeply unethical. It's structural. As of right now, **nothing illegal** has occurred. There is currently no mechanism at the Federal Election Commission to regulate AI-generated political content. No requirement that campaigns disclose altered or synthetic footage. No rules governing distribution.
The most we can be grateful for are the crowdsourced "community notes" on X. This volunteer-run fact-check system is now, absurdly, one of the only real-time defenses against electoral misinformation.
But the actual danger is the _speed_ and _willingness_ with which voters believed these videos. The visceral reactions these fakes triggered -- anger, panic, resentment, distrust -- are an unfortunate preview of how hostile the electoral terrain is about to become.
## **Fake News ' Fake News?**
However, it's not just everyday civilians getting duped by these increasingly realistic videos -- even major media players are falling for them. [Fox News was recently caught running an entire news story](https://substack.com/redirect/7adc486d-efe3-43a8-a9ed-83c311d49a09?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) based on footage they believed was real, only to discover that it was completely AI-generated.
After OpenAI released Sora 2 on September 30, social media was flooded with thousands of hyperrealistic videos created with the new text-to-video model. The explosion of content coincided with a noticeable uptick in what can only be described as "AI slop" circulating across Facebook, X, and TikTok. Some of it was harmless nonsense, but a disturbing amount followed a clear pattern.
A trend emerged showing AI-generated clips made to look like shaky iPhone videos inside grocery stores, gas stations, or fast-food restaurants, as well as mock interviews with supposed SNAP beneficiaries[. These videos depicted people bragging ](https://substack.com/redirect/b567c2a4-eee2-4b1d-b15b-0d1641316b12?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)about selling food stamps for cash or using EBT to purchase decadent meals -- all designed to "confirm" existing right-wing narratives about food stamp fraud.
Fox News took the bait.[ One video in particular,](https://substack.com/redirect/bdcce258-269b-4ec1-bf21-0c869304f7d3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) showing a woman yelling at a cashier -- "They cut my food stamps. I ain't paying for none of this s**t. I got babies at home that gotta eat." -- was treated as legitimate and turned into an entire segment pushing the idea of rampant SNAP abuse. In reality, the clip was created by several racist, right-wing influencers who have been seeding these fakes as part of a coordinated effort to inflame outrage.
But this is precisely what these AI content creators are banking on: that confirmation bias is so strong, and the videos are now so realistic, that people will believe something if it aligns with the narrative they already carry in their heads. And once they believe it, they'll share it -- spreading misinformation like an infectious disease.
The frightening part is not just that everyday people fall for these videos, but that major media outlets -- ones with the power to shape national opinion -- are being fooled, too. The line between genuine reporting and synthetic fiction is blurring, and the people producing these deepfakes know precisely how to exploit that vulnerability.
## **Artificial Plausible Deniability**
There's another side to this coin -- one that might be even more dangerous than manufactured scandals: the growing ability for people to deny real wrongdoing by blaming it on AI. As synthetic media gets more realistic, we're entering an era where anyone caught on camera or in a recording can simply shrug and say, "That wasn't me. That was AI."
AI is generating a level of distrust so pervasive that genuine evidence risks becoming meaningless. Frankly, it's surprising that Donald Trump -- the PR spinner-in-chief -- or his allies haven't leaned harder into this strategy already in response to the Jeffrey Epstein investigations. It would be easy for them to claim that photos, emails, or references connecting Epstein to Trump were artificially generated. We're likely only a few news cycles away from this exact defense.
We've already seen hints of this tactic online. After footage surfaced from the No Kings Rallies, countless right-wing accounts claimed the images were AI-generated or just altered footage from the 2017 Women's March. This emerging line of reasoning can be weaponized to delegitimize genuine public demonstrations, especially those pushing back against authoritarianism -- something we've already witnessed during Trump's presidency.
The problem is that AI detection itself is still in its infancy. The tools used to verify manipulated content are already struggling to keep pace with the new models released every few months. The technology used to fabricate reality is accelerating far faster than the technology designed to distinguish real from fake.
And that raises several critical, unsettling questions:
Will more politicians start denying criminal, corrupt, or inappropriate behavior by exploiting the public's growing distrust and claiming real evidence is synthesized?
Will citizen-led protests and grassroots movements be delegitimized -- not through propaganda, but through the insinuation that any photo or video of them is altered media?
Could we see another January 6th -- in America or abroad -- brushed aside by claiming the footage wasn't real, that rioters were AI-generated, that the chaos never happened?
These are not hypothetical risks. They are the natural progression when we allow algorithms and large language models to blur, distort, and rewrite our reality on a screen -- and when we fail to build the guardrails that protect truth itself.
## **Constructing the Guardrails**
The rapid development of Artificial Intelligence in 2025 has had a cascade of negative impacts on the country -- informational, environmental, and economic. And while Trump's executive order attempts to block a patchwork of state regulations and may lack real teeth without congressional action, what states _can_ and _should_ do is begin by regulating AI's use in politics.
Big Tech already knows the stakes. [Between 2024 and 2025,](https://substack.com/redirect/e5dacd1c-4835-41cf-909c-3f60430012bb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) Silicon Valley companies spent **$1.1 billion** on political ads and lobbying campaigns to stop these regulations before they take root. And Trump knows precisely how much influence these tech billionaires have, which is why he placed them front and center at his inauguration. Their opposition isn't subtle: meaningful regulation would cut directly into their bottom line, even at the expense of democratic stability. So they are spending enormous political and financial capital to stop it.
Despite that pressure -- and the federal government's refusal to act -- many states have already begun making significant strides. [As of 2025](https://substack.com/redirect/05e21635-f32b-4afc-9526-969cea307d1b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), **all 50 states** , Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, and Washington, D.C. have introduced legislation on AI generation. **Thirty-eight states** have adopted or enacted laws. **Twenty-three** now have statutes governing AI use in political advertising, though many are already facing legal challenges claiming they infringe on the First Amendment.
[Most of these laws ](https://substack.com/redirect/59c7f81e-ca0b-4fce-b483-52c749f49c2e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)apply only during a defined window near an election -- typically 60 to 120 days beforehand -- and specifically target "deepfake" content that falsely depicts candidates saying or doing things they never said or did. It's far from perfect, but it's a start.
One of the strongest examples comes from **[Minnesota](https://substack.com/redirect/7a657f5b-2a9b-460e-ad2a-f619c21a14bc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)** , which passed one of the most robust, enforceable deepfake statutes in the country. Under this Minnesota law, anyone who widely shares a deepfake within 90 days of an election is guilty of a crime if:
1. They **know or should have known** the content was a deepfake created without the depicted person's consent; and
2. They acted with the **intent to harm a candidate** or **influence the result** of an election.

The penalties escalate based on severity:
* **Repeat offenders (within 5 years):** Up to 5 years in prison or a fine up to $10,000, or both.
* **Intent to cause violence or bodily harm:** Up to 364 days in jail or a fine up to $3,000, or both.
* **Other cases:** Up to 90 days in jail or a fine up to $1,000, or both.

Tech industry groups are already challenging the law, but its passage underscores the seriousness of the threat -- and how deeply states recognize the damage deepfake videos can inflict on elections. When synthetic content influences outcomes, the consequences aren't abstract or hypothetical. They're immediate, real, and far-reaching. So the punishments must be equally serious.
If we genuinely want to counteract the adverse effects this technology is having on our democracy, states cannot afford half-measures. They must be willing to act boldly. These proposals should be bipartisan no-brainers -- guardrails that protect _every_ candidate, _every_ party, and _every_ voter.
And I hope that the bills the Virginia General Assembly passed before -- the very same ones Governor Youngkin vetoed -- will be taken up again, sent to Governor-elect Spanberger's desk, and signed into law, so that more candidates can't use Artificial Intelligence in bad faith like John Reid or Mike Collins already have.
Because we need these laws to be enacted. Then strengthened. Then expanded. Because the threat is not slowing down, and neither should we.
## TL;DR
AI is exploding faster than our political system can keep up -- and campaigns are already exploiting the lack of guardrails. In Virginia, a candidate even "debated" an AI-generated version of his opponent. Across the country, deepfakes are spreading misinformation, fooling voters, warping media coverage, and influencing elections with zero legal consequences. Trump's executive order blocks states from regulating AI, Big Tech is spending billions to stop reforms, and federal agencies have no mechanisms to police synthetic political content.
Some states are acting anyway, passing laws to punish deepfake misuse near elections -- but many are facing legal challenges, and Virginia's attempts were vetoed. Without bold actions in state governments, AI-driven deception will overwhelm our campaigns, our media ecosystem, and our democracy.

AI's Threat to Campaign Integrity | | | Dec 1 | | | | | | | | | | | | | | ---|---|--- , the Republican nominee for Virginia's Lieutenant Governor beamed behind a podium at an event he never thought would actually happen. After nearly 10 challenges to his opponent, Democratic Virginia State Senator Ghazala Hashmi, John Reid finally got the debate he'd been demanding for months--after being the only statewide race to not have one. The two "candidates" traded turns answering questions in what was, on its face, one of the more cordial debates of the season. Reid laid out his conservative positions on book bans, LGBTQ rights, reproductive rights, and workers' rights. Hashmi's "responses" aligned cleanly with her actual record as a Democratic nominee. The only problem was that Senator Hashmi wasn't in the room. In fact, she wasn't even participating. | | ---|---|--- Thanks for reading By the Ballot! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work. ? Hashmi was attending a stop on the "Bills Are Too Damn High" tour, an event hosted by Clean Virginia to highlight rising utility bills. She hadn't agreed to any debate at all. With only 13 days left in the cycle--and desperate to avoid being the lone candidate without a single debate--Reid's campaign unveiled what they deemed "the next best alternative."
